_comment: APPROXIMATE reference curves for the keystroke-response-time CDF from the
Mosh paper, for visual context only — NOT digitized point-for-point from the
figure. The shapes reproduce the paper's qualitative result and are pinned to
its quoted headline statistics; replace the points below with exact digitized
values if you need a precise overlay.
Source: Keith Winstein and Hari Balakrishnan, 'Mosh: An Interactive Remote
Shell for Mobile Clients', USENIX ATC 2012 (Figure 2, 'keystroke response
time'). Quoted: over a commercial EV-DO (3G) network, Mosh displayed ~70% of
keystrokes instantly via predictive local echo, with a median keystroke
response time < 5 ms, versus 503 ms for SSH.
Each curve is a list of [response_ms, cumulative_fraction] points defining a
CDF (fraction of keystrokes displayed within response_ms).
citation: Winstein & Balakrishnan, "Mosh", USENIX ATC 2012 (Fig. 2) — approximate, illustrative
network: commercial EV-DO (3G); Mosh median <5 ms, SSH median 503 ms
